transcript.text: How are you?
This meeting is being recorded.
I'm great.
How was your holiday break?
It's good.
It's good.
Stay local in NYC?
Traveling where?
Went to Poconos area to take the kids a little bit snowboarding, but that's kind of about it.
Not too far.
Okay, nice.
Yeah.
I've been to Tahoe a bunch, skiing, snowboarding.
How is it in the Poconos area?
I have no idea what it's like.
Not like that.
This is Northeast, so snow sucks.
But, you know, I took the kids, and they're like three and six, so it's fine.
Probably the three-year-old's first time ever doing that, I would imagine.
Yeah, exactly.
That's, man, I feel like three years old and snowboarding, if you can accomplish that, you can pretty much accomplish anything.
You accomplish much, I will tell you that, but the six-year-old liked it.
Okay, nice, nice.
Well, great to meet you.
Excited to chat, GrowthX.
man.
And had a bunch of good conversations with Jesse.
So happy to hear that he mentioned us.
Have you worked with him at Tavis?
We are about to finalize an agreement.
And I actually have a call with Jesse this afternoon.
Okay.
I don't know him that well, but I have a friend that worked at Tavis, I met him.
And obviously, I know he came from RAM.
And I think you guys work with him, too.
So I heard some good things.
I was, to be honest, I didn't hear too much besides, like, I should talk to you guys.
And I don't know if I fully understand what you guys do.
So I think excited to kind of learn a little bit more.
Yeah, definitely.
Let's start there.
Definitely a good place to kick off the conversation.
Think of GrowthX as, like, an extension of your content team.
And we work end-to-end.
So, like, an agency would work.
But we have some products that we're launching, like, really soon.
And we've got this platform that powers all of our work.
And so what we're doing is, like, we're helping you create content optimized for SEO and AEO.
And then we're also coming up with the initial content strategy and then doing all of the monitoring and visibility around, like, where you're showing up in AI search.
Does that clear up, like, what we do a bit better?
I want to make sure that's personal before we go on.
How do you differ than Aerobs, which I will be honest here, we just signed a contract with and we just launched with.
Okay.
Yeah, no, totally understand.
So I would say it's definitely like a decision, right?
Like, do you want to use an Aerobs or do you want to use a growthx?
And how we differ is that we have basically built everything and can fine tune the engine so that you can get results quicker.
And then some teams, right, like they use Aerobs and they build these workflows themselves, but maybe they're not able to get the content to the quality that they're looking to hit or...
Generate the performance, right, that they're looking to drive.
So I can definitely go deeper into how we work.
And there are some scenarios like where a company uses aerox internally and growthx.
But I feel like that's typically in like an enterprise situation, not a startup situation.
So let me know, I mean, what would be most helpful for you today?
And we can kind of go from there.
similar, I think, to aerox.
But I think with like, yes, like we will build the workflows ourselves.
We'll work with them to do that.
And you guys kind of like, obviously, have learned how to do it yourself.
If it is basically kind of like that with content creation, workflow building, and AI and SEO optimization, I'm going to be honest, we probably have that with aerox.
And I don't think we can.
Yeah, yeah.
No, I understand.
And I guess what were you looking for in like, sort of setting up this conversation, obviously, to learn more about us, but like, what problem was this connected to?
Or gap that, you know, Aerox wasn't already solving?
Well, we just signed with Aerox.
So I was looking for something kind of like this in terms of basically how do we, I think, accelerate our content production.
We don't have a content team internally.
And I do think one of the pluses, I think, with AI is maybe we have freelancers, but, like, I don't know.
need to necessarily – that's my number one hire at the moment where, like, you know, like three years ago, that probably would be.
So I was looking for, I think, AI-powered content workflows, acceleration of pieces of content that we're writing, and then also, obviously, ranking for both SEO and AI SEO.
Obviously, they're still related, right?
So that is what I was looking for, which in theory is, I think, your Aerox.
Aerox or GrowthX would help?
Yeah, different flavors of that.
Yeah.
The reason why I was, like, I was reading, I was, like, I'm also looking.
I think for, I think trying to talk to people who do either go to market engineering in terms of different, I think, areas in terms of how we can scale that.
And maybe people who are helping write with such strategic content, but like not necessarily about SEO, AEO, or anything like that, which I don't think is you guys.
Yeah, that definitely wouldn't be like our core service and like why we'd sign a new agreement, but we do get pretty flexible over the partnership, right?
Like if we've done a bunch of blog content and we've refreshed a lot of content, we'll like look for a different place where we can plug in.
So maybe what I can do is I can just give you like a quick five-minute spiel just so you sort of understand like the deeper differentiation.

And then I'm not sure, you know, what the contract with Aerox is like, but if there is any sort of budget flexibility or like it's not going the way you want and you want to look at a different option, then you've got the context from today.
Yep.
Cool.
I'll share a couple slides, show you a couple examples, and we'll kind of give each other some time back, which will be great.
So yeah, again, strategy to execution and analytics.
And then we also have this healthy combination of like experts in the loop for every step of the workflows that are involved in creating content.

And so, you know, like one reason that a lot of teams are confident working with us, we've been growing really quickly is our CEO and founder has built organic content engines at like every step in his career.
And so like, whether it was IBM in the early social media days, he built this micro site that was like their highest ROI and marketing channel, you know, scale.
He was the CMO through the acquisition by Meta, Hashtag Corp.
He was in that hyper growth period from six to a hundred million.
And then what he did basically starting growth X was at deep gram, he used air ops and he built really complicated workflows.
To automate the process where he could.
And the results were like 24x their traffic in 18 months, 4x their revenue.
And so Marcel, you'll see on LinkedIn, like he's very much a build in public kind of CEO.
And when he was at Deepgram, was hosting these workshops.
And that's what led to the start of Growthx was like people wanted help.
And the main problem is, you know, how do I get from like zero to one with this tool in a way that's driving higher intent traffic?
And so I think it'll be interesting, right?
Like it's always good to build in-house and sort of learn if that's the right fit for you, if you've got the benefit of time on your side.

But a lot of companies, you know, they choose us because they don't want to spend that time on the learning curve or they want a partner who's not just doing like strategy and sending some briefs, right?
We publish every business day.
So that's a core part of like what we do is create quality at scale.
Take a quick breath.
Take a sip of water.
But any questions?
Do you have for me so far as we're going through?
No, I think that makes sense.
High level, how expensive are you guys?
Since, like, obviously there's, like, all, like, the hands-on support.
I think the pricing on the site starts at, like, $18.
Is that accurate or, like, just kind of, like, an anchor myself?
Yeah, for sure.
$18 is, like, you know, the list price and then we can negotiate a bit from there.
We don't really ever go below $15 per month.

Just because if you think about, like, the cost of creating this quantity through any alternative, it's going to be, you know, above $15,000, especially for articles that are, like, really designed to drive results quickly.
Yep, I understand.
I think either way we probably would have ended up with just air ops from a budget perspective.
I do understand, like, I think the value of someone who's done it and understands where, like, we are trying to figure it out.
So I think maybe if it's not working or scaling, but we have at least proven that investing in this is a growth driver and we want to accelerate, maybe it might make sense to reach back out.
Yeah, totally.
I think that's a good plan.
And I would say like in your space, we've worked with a lot of great names and really understand how to write content while there.
So like Ramp, Brex, they're, I don't know if you know Turnstile, do know Turnstile?
No.
Were you working with Metronome?
We still work with Metronome.
Yep.
It's mainly like B2B software companies that some, you know, have like a PLG and enterprise, some are just enterprise.
And then Lovable is like a good consumer example.
But when you think about like what we do for these companies, you know, a lot of times it's editorial content to start off.
So for augment code, we've created like.
Like 300 guides, including all the image generation that you see here.
We'll cross link, we'll get really tailored with like what we create.
So they have a lot of code snippets and their content.
And then just like a quick look at some of the results.
So, you know, they want to be known as like the best enterprise AI coding tool.
And they compete against cursor, GitHub.
What's cool about this is GitHub is obviously much larger.
And augment code articles that we've written are cited, you know, in their bullets.
They're also, you know, listed above cursor, which is great.
And you can see that the cursor example also has the augment code article cited.
You'll see this, you know, in a lot of different scenarios with searching augment code.
We've got all these different articles that we've created, you know, showing up.
And I know it's a bit biased, right, to my computer and what I've searched in the past.
But, you know, we positioned.
companies in a way that they really want to be seen, right?
Like, Augment Code has a huge context window, and they can handle huge code bases.
And so they want to, you know, show up in a way that they're not just the leading tool, but they're also positioned as being designed for the enterprise.
And then when you look at, you know, just their organic traffic growth, we started working with them in around June when they were 20K monthly.
And now they're over 3X, right?
Six months later, completely shutting off their paid ad spend.
So we'll start off on, like, more editorial content.
With Ramp, we've published over 700 pages.
And then we'll move to different projects that, like, make sense, right?
So for Ramp, it was the spender directory.
And it was a programmatic play.
We leveraged their internal spend data.
And now we've created, like, these really powerful tool reviews.
And, you know, they want to be seen as, like, the authority with anything that's business spending.
And so we're pretty versatile.

was like a partner, but we always start off in the area that is going to drive visibility, which is creating, right, quality at scale or editorial for listicles, definitions, how-tos, comparisons, really like, like you said, right, accelerating how fast you can create the content that's driving traffic today.
I think you have some, like, really good examples on the website of the kind of content that we would be able to build out in a bunch of different angles.
Yep.
Okay.
Makes a lot of sense.
Okay.
Perfect.
Yeah.
No, I think, I think I just wanted to give you the full rundown in an accelerated fashion.
And, you know, if it makes sense to dive deeper, we can go into the actual, like, workflows and I can sort of, like, go under the hood of how are they created, right?
Not make it so much of a black box as, you know, they use AI and I don't understand how they use AI.
But, you know,
For now, think, I hope your team is able to find, you know, success, right?
Like using their own platform.
Yeah, I think this is probably maybe like steps of different things.
We did use an AI tool that just mass generated content.
It was very successful at mass generating content, not successful at ranking for anything.
So we have moved on from that to one that will use AI to generate content, but is more thoughtful and structured.
So I think maybe I'll keep you in mind as we, you know, figure out what works and what doesn't.
But very good to learn and thanks for walking me through.
Yeah, you bet.
Give you 10 minutes back in your day and I'll let you know too, we've got like a community that has like over a thousand marketing and growth leaders.
So I'll send you access to that.
And we're also launching a product that's more like AI visibility.
So think like profound, if you haven't made your decision there, I'll let you know when that launches.
Maybe you can tell me what is your.
What are your, like, do you guys use Profound or how do you, like, Profound is very, was very hot, but they're just a monitoring tool.
So, like, can I get that data anywhere?
No, we would, we would definitely agree with you.
We use Scrunch as our partner just for setting up a dashboard of tracking prompts and citations and sentiment.
But we're launching our own product next month that we think has a lot, you know, better bells and whistles in terms of, like, not starting from zero on which prompts.
It's track, things like that.
Yeah.
And is that only for your other customers?
Is that going to be, like, a SaaS tool that people can sign up for or?
Yeah, it's going to be both.
It's going to be part of, like, the White Glove service engagements.
And then it's also going to be, like, something that you can pay for and sign up for.
So, when that's launched, I'll definitely let you know.
Yeah, because we talked to Profound, too.
We're not going with Profound because I think they're overpriced.
And I don't think they have a moat on what they're doing, which is basically.
I can monitor our rankings.
hard part is not monitoring.
The hard part is content creation.
Right.
The coach is going to tell me my ranking sucks, obviously.
And I think there's enough places that has insight in how to rank with AI.
not a black box.
GPT tells you where they're pulling sources from publicly.
So I think that's my perspective in terms of tools like Profound.
My main challenge is content creation and how do we actually rank and how do we accelerate that, right?
Not necessarily the visibility tracking.
feel like there's many tools that can do that in different ways.
You're definitely saying the same thing we believe here.
Yeah.
But no, I'm glad we got a chance to connect.
And I'll send you those resources and updates when they're relevant.
And yeah, I mean, feel free to reach out to me if anything comes up soon.
Great.
Thank you.
Bye.
Thanks, Louise.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[GrowthX intro call to explore AI content acceleration services.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=233.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Securitas Healthcare just signed with Aerobs for its content acceleration needs, making a GrowthX partnership redundant.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=139.0)
  - [GrowthX is a full-service agency (strategy, execution, analytics) that uses a proprietary platform and expert-in-the-loop workflows to deliver higher-quality, faster-ranking content than self-service tools.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=371.0)
  - [GrowthX's service is priced at \~$15k/mo, which is significantly higher than Securitas's current budget for Aerobs.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=485.0)
  - [Securitas will monitor its Aerobs results and may reconsider GrowthX if it needs to accelerate content production further.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=526.0)

## Topics

### Securitas's Content Needs & Current Solution

  - [**Primary Goal:** Accelerate content production for SEO & AI SEO.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=233.0)
  - [**Internal Resources:** No content team; relies on freelancers.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=245.0)
  - [**Current Solution:** Just signed a contract with Aerobs.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=139.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Securitas will build its own workflows on the platform, a more budget-friendly approach than a full-service agency.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=203.0)
  - [**Past Experience:** A previous AI tool failed to rank content, reinforcing the need for a thoughtful, structured approach.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=785.0)

### GrowthX's Differentiated Service Model

  - [**Model:** A full-service agency combining a proprietary platform with expert-in-the-loop workflows.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=106.0)
  - [**Value Proposition:** Delivers higher-quality, faster-ranking content than self-service tools by eliminating the client's learning curve and workflow-building effort.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=450.0)
  - [**Leadership:** Founded by Marcel, who built Deepgram's content engine (24x traffic, 4x revenue) using Aerobs, then created GrowthX to solve the "zero-to-one" problem for other companies.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=383.0)
  - [**Pricing:** \~$15k/mo minimum, reflecting the cost of creating high-quality, results-driven content at scale.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=485.0)

### GrowthX Case Studies

  - [**Augment Code:**](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=590.0)
      - [**Goal:** Establish enterprise AI coding tool authority.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=612.0)
      - [**Action:** Created \>300 guides.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=590.0)
      - [**Result:** 3x organic traffic in 6 months; articles outrank competitors (Cursor, GitHub) and are cited in their content.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=612.0)
  - [**Ramp:**](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=692.0)
      - [**Action:** Published \>700 pages.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=692.0)
      - [**Result:** Expanded from editorial content to programmatic projects (e.g., Spender Directory) using internal data, establishing Ramp as a business spending authority.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=692.0)

### AI Visibility Tooling

  - [**Securitas's View:** Visibility tracking tools (e.g., Profound) are overpriced and lack a moat. The core challenge is content creation and ranking, not just monitoring.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=838.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Response:**](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=838.0)
      - [Agrees with this assessment.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=838.0)
      - [Uses Scrunch for internal tracking but is launching its own tool next month.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=838.0)
      - [**New Tool:** Will offer "better bells and whistles" and a head start on tracking prompts/citations.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=851.0)
      - [**Availability:** Will be a SaaS product and integrated into white-glove service engagements.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=872.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Tyler:** Send Louise an invite to the GrowthX community (\>1,000 marketing leaders).](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=819.0)
  - [**Tyler:** Notify Louise when the new AI visibility product launches.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=829.0)
  - [**Louise:** Monitor Aerobs performance; reconnect with GrowthX if a more accelerated content strategy is needed.](https://fathom.video/share/1BHXDVXqfqVm4jhNJxdxVTs-xegeH79m?tab=summary&timestamp=526.0)
